title: Notocactus alacriportanus
taxonomy: Notocactus alacriportanus is a species of cactus native to Brazil, previously classified under the genus Parodia. The classification of Notocactus alacriportanus has undergone changes over time. In the context of modern botanical nomenclature, it belongs to the family Cactaceae. However, its classification can also be expressed within the broader group of subfamily Cactoideae. This species shares its classification with other cacti within the same genera and subfamily.

intro: Notocactus alacriportanus, also known as the "Blue Barrel Cactus," is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae, native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Notocactus alacriportanus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Notocactus alacriportanus is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is commonly used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory disorders.
